operating_state: Total revenues increased to $49.6 billion in Q2 2024 from $47.2 billion in Q2 2023, driven by higher upstream realizations and sales volumes that offset lower downstream margins. Net oil-equivalent production averaged 3.32 million barrels per day in the first six months, up 12 percent from prior year primarily due to PDC Energy acquisition and production growth in Permian and Denver-Julesburg Basins. Upstream earnings declined to $4.5 billion from $4.9 billion quarterly while downstream earnings fell sharply due to compressed refining margins.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased reflecting higher upstream operational costs and employee expenses from PDC acquisition integration. Depreciation, depletion and amortization rose to $4.0 billion from $3.5 billion quarterly, primarily driven by higher production volumes and increased rates. The effective tax rate increased to 37% quarterly from 23% in the prior year due to absence of favorable prior period items and jurisdictional mix effects.
material_events: PDC Energy acquisition successfully integrated, contributing to record Permian Basin production and substantial U.S. upstream volume increases. The company paid quarterly dividends of $1.63 per share totaling $3.0 billion and completed $3.0 billion in share repurchases during Q2 2024, with $6.0 billion repurchased in the first six months. Capital expenditures increased for the six-month period largely from post-acquisition PDC asset investments. Tengizchevroil achieved start-up of Wellhead Pressure Management Project in April 2024.
macro_exposures: The company faces ongoing climate change litigation across multiple U.S. jurisdictions seeking damages related to fossil fuel production plus Louisiana coastal erosion cases. Operations continue in Israel despite regional conflict while political unrest in Bangladesh and Venezuelan operations following July presidential election present uncertain impacts. Natural gas price volatility significantly impacted upstream margins with U.S. natural gas realizations declining 38% year-over-year.
forward_outlook: Management expects third quarter share repurchases between $4.0-$4.75 billion under the ongoing program with $57.8 billion remaining authorization. Future Growth Project at Tengizchevroil expected to start up in first half of 2025 while the company maintains 2050 net zero upstream aspiration and carbon intensity targets. The company maintains its commitment to the pending Hess Corporation acquisition, subject to regulatory approvals and resolution of ongoing arbitration proceedings.
uncertainty_profile: Hess Corporation merger faces FTC review expected to conclude in third quarter 2024 and Stabroek Block arbitration hearing scheduled for May 2025, potentially delaying or preventing transaction completion. Pending climate litigation and coastal cases present material uncertainty given their unprecedented nature and inability to estimate liability ranges. OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax framework effective in various jurisdictions with evaluation ongoing.
